steps.title: Use subtraction to obtain 3x plus 3y
steps.content: Instead of solving for $x$ and $y$ individually, we can obtain $3x + 3y$ directly by subtracting Equation 2 from Equation 1. This works because $(8 - 5) = 3$ and $(-4 - (-7)) = 3$, so the $x$ and $y$ coefficients will both be $3$.

steps.title: Subtract the equations and simplify
steps.content:
Compute Equation 1 minus Equation 2:
$$(8x - 4y) - (5x - 7y) = 6 - 14$$
$$3x + 3y = -8$$
B is correct.
